checks.finding: All task Requirements references resolve to AC-001 through AC-007 defined in acceptance-tests.md.
Every task T-001 through T-008 has a Blockers field containing either None or comma-separated valid T-NNN identifiers; precheck-result.json also records blockers_format_valid=true.
REQ-001 through REQ-011 are each referenced by at least one task Requirements field.
AC-001 through AC-007 are covered by T-001/T-002, T-004/T-005, T-003, T-003, T-006, T-007, and T-008 respectively.
Every task T-001 through T-008 references at least one valid REQ-NNN identifier in its Requirements field.

No task is titled as or scoped exclusively to test/spec/verification work; testing in T-001 through T-008 is directly attached to implementation or release concerns whose Requirements fields include AC identifiers.
In attempt 1 round 3, all tasks T-001 through T-008 retain Approval: Draft and Status: Planned; none is self-approved.
T-001, T-006, T-007, and T-008 pair medium with acceptance-first; T-002 through T-005 pair high with tdd. precheck-result.json records workflow_match_precheck=PASS.

Shared AC-001 is differentiated between T-001 capability/role-floor scope and T-002 selection/escalation scope; shared AC-002 is differentiated between T-004 implementation-agent scope and T-005 reviewer/evaluator scope. AC-003 through AC-007 have unique task targets.
Every blocker reference resolves to one of dependency-graph.json nodes T-001 through T-008.
The dependency graph is acyclic: dependencies progress from T-001 through T-002/T-003, T-004/T-005, T-006, T-007, and T-008 without revisiting a node.
Each task has one coherent concern: capability policy, routing/resume, manifest integrity, implementation isolation, review isolation, reporting metrics, release synchronization, or atomic rollback.

Each task names inspectable evidence: TEST-001 through TEST-006, exact red/green log and implementation-report paths for high-risk tasks, explicit Bash/PowerShell outcomes, or exact run-all and repository-validator commands. Remaining criteria state binary routing, isolation, release, or rollback outcomes.

traceability.md's Layer Coverage assigns REQ-004 through REQ-007 to Security and REQ-008 through REQ-011 to Infrastructure, but every corresponding requirement row declares 'N/A — cross-layer only' instead of a canonical hash-bound layer-spec anchor. This is substantively contradicted by security-spec.md coverage of terminal recurrence and manifest/session isolation and infra-spec.md coverage of deterministic-runtime, release, and rollback behavior. Consequently downstream implementation cannot trace these declared layer obligations to the reviewed layer artifacts; this is a blocking structural inconsistency.
